decision.semantic_admission_delta: positive for focused DirectMethod Promise producer fixtures; unchanged on the local crates runtime surface because crates has no JS/TS Promise continuation rows
decision.summary: Open only the proof-backed DirectMethod subset of the member call-return Promise receiver queue. A method call can become PromiseLike receiver proof only when an existing DirectMethod call-target record points to a non-async single-return body and the returned expression already has asserted PromiseLike domain evidence.
policy: A member call result used as `.then`/`.catch` receiver is not Promise proof by itself.
The kernel does not infer DirectMethod evidence from selector spelling or object shape.
DirectMethod call-result domain proof depends on both the DirectMethod call-target record and the returned expression's PromiseLike domain record.
The value graph evaluates only the returned expression and closes if that expression reads receiver context such as `this`, `super`, or `self`.
The recovered continuation remains behind a Promise boundary and must not converge with a synchronous payload.

corpus_source_pricing.scan_condition: 120-repo JS/TS lexical source scan for .then/.catch/.finally call-return receivers
limitations: The source scan is a pricing signal, not proof of semantic recovery in every source occurrence.
This slice only benefits member calls whose frontend or semantic pack already supplies DirectMethod target evidence.
Ordinary object member calls, dynamic dispatch, imported members, parameter callees, and missing return-domain proof remain closed.

focused_regression_coverage: DirectMethod call-target evidence plus returned-expression Domain(PromiseLike) evidence emits call-result Domain(PromiseLike) with both dependencies.
DirectMethod call-target evidence without returned-expression Domain(PromiseLike) evidence emits no call-result PromiseLike proof.
A proven DirectMethod single-return method returning Promise.resolve(1) feeds `.then` recovery and stays distinct from synchronous `1 + 1`.
A proven DirectMethod return expression that reads receiver context stays opaque even when the returned expression is otherwise PromiseLike.
A source-level member call-return receiver such as `service.load().then(...)` remains closed without DirectMethod target and return-domain proof.
closed_boundaries_after_slice: methods whose returned expression reads `this`, `super`, or `self`
async method scheduling and rejection paths
dynamic dispatch and imported member calls
possible thenable assimilation through unsafe Promise.resolve arguments
Promise constructors, `.finally`, aggregate combinators, and broad scheduling equivalence
next_work: Price which JS/TS frontends or packs can safely emit DirectMethod evidence for static/private/final or otherwise concrete member calls.
Split imported-member Promise call-return receivers separately; they are 105 occurrences across 9 repos and need import-coordinate plus return-domain proof.
Keep ordinary object/member selectors reporting-only until receiver identity and method target proof are available.
